coins.title: Coins
coins.noCoins: No coins in this collection yet.
coins.noCoinsHint: Click "Add Coin" to start your collection.
coins.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this coin?
coins.llmExportTooltip: Paste this JSON into an AI chat and ask your questions — e.g. about current market prices, mintage, etc. See the prompt template below for an example. Important: the response must contain "id" and "info" fields only.
coins.llmImportTooltip: Copy the JSON response from the AI chat into a file, then import it here to add the information to your coins.
coins.llmPrompt:
You are a professional numismatist-expert. For each coin in the JSON below, add useful information to the "info" field. USE WEB SEARCH if available on your platform to find accurate data.

SEARCH PLAN for each coin:
1. Identify the coin precisely: metal composition (gold/silver/copper fineness), weight (grams), diameter (mm), edge type, catalog numbers (KM#, Y#, Conros, Bitkin).
2. Find the EXACT catalog number — search "[year] [denomination] [country] KM#" on numismatic sites (NGC, Numista, Krause). Verify the number belongs to THIS coin, not a similar one.
3. Find RECENT (2025-2026) market prices from: eBay sold listings, Heritage Auctions, Stack's Bowers, Katz Auctions, Violity, Wolmar.
4. For silver/gold coins: calculate melt value (metal spot price × pure weight). Use as minimum price floor.
5. Quote price as a RANGE with currency: "500-800 RUB (~$6-9)". For Russian coins, primary in RUB.

CRITICAL RULES:
- Do NOT invent catalog numbers or prices — if search yields nothing, state "no data available".
- Do NOT confuse denominations: crown ≠ half-crown, 5 kopeks copper ≠ 5 kopeks silver, ruble ≠ kopeck.
- Indicate confidence level if uncertain.
- Include mintage figures where known (verify with Krause or mint records).
- List notable varieties (mint marks, edge varieties, die varieties).

IMPORTANT: Output ONLY a valid JSON array containing only the coins you modified. Do NOT include coins you haven't changed. Do NOT add explanations, markdown, or extra text outside the JSON. Each object must have "id" (matching the original coin) and "info" (your analysis text).

Here is my coin collection:
{{json}}

Example response format:
[
  { "id": "abc-123", "info": "KM# 765. Silver 925, 28.28g. London Mint. Mintage: 1,807,200. Common in VF, scarce in UNC. Market price: $80-150 VF+ (eBay 2025-2026 sold listings)." },
  { "id": "def-456", "info": "Rare variety with wide edge lettering. Only ~50K minted. Price: 5000-8000₽ (UNC)." }
]
